title: Welcome to Go!
description: Discover what Go is and why it's so popular.

slides.title: What is Go? 🐹
slides.content:
## Meet the Gopher!

**Go**, also known as *Golang*, is an open-source programming language created by Google in 2007.

It was designed to be:
* **Simple:** Easy to read and write.
* **Fast:** Compiles quickly into machine code.
* **Efficient:** Great at running multiple tasks at once (we'll learn this later!).

Go's mascot is a cute Gopher!

quizData.question: Which company created the Go programming language?
quizData.explanation: Go was designed at Google in 2007 by Robert Griesemer, Rob Pike, and Ken Thompson to improve programming productivity.

slides.title: Why Learn Go? 🚀
slides.content:
## Built for the Cloud

Go is incredibly popular for building **backend systems**, **cloud infrastructure** (like Docker and Kubernetes), and **web servers**.

Why?
Because it combines the performance of languages like C/C++ with the simplicity and readability of Python.

* No more waiting minutes for code to compile.
* No more complex syntax that looks like alien math.

quizData.question: Go is often praised because it combines:

options.text: Performance of C++ and simplicity of Python
Browser compatibility of JavaScript and styling of CSS
Complexity of Assembly and slowness of Ruby

quizData.explanation: Go is loved because it's fast (like C++) but very easy to read and understand (like Python).

slides.title: The Statically Typed Nature 🧱
slides.content:
## Catch Bugs Early

Go is **statically typed**. This simply means that when you decide a piece of data is a number, it stays a number forever! You can't suddenly treat it like text.

This helps the Go compiler catch many potential bugs *before* your program even runs! Think of it like a spellchecker for your code.

quizData.question: Go is a 'statically typed' language. True or False?
quizData.explanation: Go is statically typed, meaning variable types are known at compile time, leading to safer code.
